message:
fix(html_export): harden JS initialization and search/popover behavior

Multiple robustness improvements to the JavaScript layer:

- Search.init: dynamically create search-count element if missing from
  DOM, preventing null reference errors in encrypted exports where the
  element may not exist until decryption completes
- Search.search: widen search scope to .message elements (falling back
  to .message-content) so text within tool badges and other nested
  elements is also searchable
- ToolPopovers.initBadges: guard against double-binding with a
  data-popoverBound flag, preventing duplicate event listeners when
  init is called multiple times (e.g., after decryption)
- ToolPopovers.hide: reset inline position styles when hiding to
  prevent stale fixed-position coordinates on re-show
- ToolPopovers.position: set position:fixed on the popover element
  to ensure viewport-relative positioning works correctly
- Crypto.decrypt: re-initialize Search, ToolPopovers, and WorldClass
  after decryption so all features work on decrypted content
- generate_init_js: wrap each module init in try/catch to prevent one
  module's failure from blocking all others; use readyState check with
  immediate execution fallback instead of always deferring to
  DOMContentLoaded (handles cases where DOM is already ready)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
